recap: Erling Haaland's 86th-minute finish capped Norway's 2-1 victory over Ivory Coast, sending them to the round of 16 after Patrick Berg drew three defenders before finding him unmarked in the box. Antonio Nusa had given Norway the lead with a curling strike, but Amad Diallo equalized in the 74th minute with a left-footed penalty-box shot before Haaland's late heroics secured Norway's first-ever World Cup knockout win. The result sets up a stunning rematch against Brazil on Sunday, 28 years after Norway's legendary 2-1 group-stage upset of the then-defending champions in 1998.
